element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Player chooses from 16 generic teams based on 1990 NFL season
Various field conditions available before play begins
Call audibles to adjust play at line of scrimmage
Unique above-and-behind QB viewpoint rather than side view; requires spatial reorientation
Bonus squad with superior stats available after unlocking

rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Match pacing governed by real football rhythm with play-clock management; arcade action emphasis creates faster decision-making than simulation-focused competitors

difficulty_curve.early_game: Accessible arcade-style entry; player learns QB perspective and basic play calling
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Increased competitive pressure; opponent AI adapts to player strategies
difficulty_curve.late_game: Championship/All-Madden difficulty emphasizes audible system mastery and field condition adaptation
notable_spikes: First match adjustment to above-behind QB camera angle
All-Madden team difficulty spike when unlocked

notable_level_patterns.description: Individual game with pre-game setup, play-calling sequences, and post-game results; no narrative progression or level gates
notable_level_patterns.example_level: Generic team vs. Generic team matchup

notable_level_patterns.description: Multi-game campaign mode where consecutive wins unlock stronger opponents and All-Madden team
Unique QB perspective (above-and-behind) serves both as spatial identifier and learning curve—viewpoint itself is gameplay element requiring mastery

sources_used: wikipedia - John Madden Football (1990 video game)
